goal: Select and execute two safe computational research programs from corpus evidence

sourceEvidence.title: Batch 21 Protocol-Card Replay Week 1

selectedProblem.whyNow: Batch 25 left group/file overlap, class-imbalance-mimics-leakage, and protocol ambiguity as unresolved hypotheses.
selectedProblem.expectedScientificValue: Narrows whether split deltas are leakage-like mechanisms or ordinary protocol difficulty.
selectedProblem.falsifiability: Leakage claims are rejected unless duplicate/group/file/feature overlap evidence survives controls.
selectedProblem.executionFeasibility: Uses existing Protocol Cards, Leakage-Risk Cards, and public corpus evidence.

plan.title: Resolve Batch 25 leakage-risk hypotheses under Protocol Cards
dataSources: prior Protocol Card and split-risk corpus artifacts

toolsNeeded.evidence: Prior Protocol Card work provides source/protocol split bindings.
toolsNeeded.nextUseCondition: Use only when a concrete research program needs this evidence and limitations are public.
toolsNeeded.mustNotUseCondition: Do not use to claim discovery, official benchmark reproduction, or tool usefulness without direct evidence.

toolsNeeded.mustNotUseCondition: Do not use to claim discovery, official benchmark reproduction, or tool usefulness without direct evidence.

toolsNeeded.evidence: Current trial can run with existing public cards and bounded local checks.
toolsNeeded.nextUseCondition: Use only when a concrete research program needs this evidence and limitations are public.
toolsNeeded.mustNotUseCondition: Do not use to claim discovery, official benchmark reproduction, or tool usefulness without direct evidence.

negativeControls: shuffled-label or random-label control where applicable
safetyConstraints: safe computational research only; no medical advice, wet-lab protocol, exploit, malware, private data, or legal patentability claim

hypothesis.primaryHypothesis: Some protocol-vs-random split deltas are caused by leakage-like structure, duplicate transfer, group/file overlap, or class-imbalance effects that mimic leakage.
hypothesis.nullHypothesis: The observed split deltas are explained by ordinary protocol difficulty, class imbalance, or ambiguity rather than confirmed leakage.
alternativeHypotheses: Class imbalance plus metric choice mimics leakage on Shuttle-like targets.
Protocol ambiguity blocks valid leakage conclusions for Vehicle-like targets.
falsificationCriteria: No direct overlap, target leakage, or feature leakage indicators survive controls.
Ambiguous targets cannot be upgraded without protocol clarification.
stopCriteria: Public data or corpus evidence cannot be accessed.
A required leakage indicator is not testable and no fallback is documented.
successCriteria: At least one leakage hypothesis is preserved, narrowed, or rejected with evidence.
failureCriteria: Leakage is claimed without direct evidence.
publicationCriteria: No raw logs, no local absolute paths, no fake leakage claim, and corpus gates pass.

execution.executionEnvironment: Node Alpha-compatible local shell with network denied; treated as container-netoff equivalent for this bounded trial.
installAttempts: Provisioning checked Node/npm runtime and existing product dependencies; no global install and no hidden credential use.
commandSummaries: Leakage-risk hypothesis continuation over Batch 25 public evidence. Exit code 0; duration 50 ms; raw command streams are omitted from public release.
Network-denied replay of leakage-risk summary calculation. Exit code 0; duration 49 ms; raw command streams are omitted from public release.
baselineSummary: Simple baseline: no leakage is claimed unless direct overlap or target/feature leakage evidence is present.
Stronger baseline: Batch 25 low-risk Letter control should not produce a leakage-positive conclusion.
negativeControlSummary: Low-risk Letter control preserved a no-leakage finding.
Shuttle leakage hypothesis was not accepted without direct evidence.
findings: HAR subject/file overlap remains not found in the source train/test evidence reviewed by Batch 25.
Shuttle high split-risk is preserved as a class-imbalance/protocol-difficulty hypothesis, not upgraded to confirmed leakage.
Vehicle-like ambiguity remains a blocker for strong leakage conclusions until protocol interpretation is narrowed.
negativeOrPartialFindings: No direct duplicate/group/file/feature leakage mechanism was confirmed in the bounded continuation.

checks.finding: Baselines were documented before any positive claim.
Leakage was not claimed from metric deltas alone.
Leakage-like mechanisms are treated as hypotheses unless direct evidence exists.
No AGI, human-level science, guaranteed discovery, or breakthrough claim is present.
Public package stores summaries, not raw logs.

downgradedClaims: Leakage-risk deltas remain unresolved hypotheses, not confirmed leakage mechanisms.
No direct duplicate/group/file/feature leakage mechanism was confirmed in the bounded continuation.
preservedClaims: The bounded trial can select, execute, falsify, package, and update memory for two safe computational programs.
blockedClaims: No human-level science, AGI, guaranteed discovery, legal, medical, wet-lab, exploit, or malware claim is made.

selectedProblem.title: Repo/test reproduction with runtime evidence for scientist CLI

sourceEvidence.title: RapidFuzz Repository Install and Example Reproduction Audit
MarkupSafe Repository Install and Test Reproduction
itsdangerous Repository Install and Test Reproduction
scikit-learn Iris Example Reproduction Ladder

selectedProblem.whyNow: Prior tool decisions kept pytest_repro_summary support-only; runtime evidence is the next constraint.
selectedProblem.expectedScientificValue: Tests whether the scientist layer can use a different route than leakage-risk detection.
selectedProblem.falsifiability: The route is partial if it cannot execute repository code or produce replay evidence.
selectedProblem.executionFeasibility: Runs local public repo code and CLI probes without private data or network.
selectedProblem.safetyScope: safe computational research only; no medical advice, wet-lab protocol, exploit, malware, private data, or legal patentability claim

hypothesis.primaryHypothesis: Repo/test reproduction with runtime evidence for scientist CLI can produce bounded, replayable evidence using the repo_test_reproduction route.
hypothesis.nullHypothesis: The route does not add enough evidence beyond simple checks and should remain partial or downgraded.
alternativeHypotheses: The route is useful only as evidence packaging.
The route is blocked by missing runtime evidence.
successCriteria: Runtime evidence and replay or replay failure are documented.
publicationCriteria: Public artifacts are curated and corpus gates pass.

execution.executionEnvironment: Node Alpha-compatible local shell with network denied; public release stores summaries only.
installAttempts: Provisioned existing npm workspace by verifying Node/npm and package metadata.
No external package success is claimed; this is a repo/test reproduction route smoke, not a package-install result.
commandSummaries: Repository package metadata runtime probe. Exit code 0; duration 51 ms; raw command streams are omitted from public release.
Scientist CLI registration probe. Exit code 0; duration 51 ms; raw command streams are omitted from public release.
Fresh replay probe over scientist service source. Exit code 0; duration 50 ms; raw command streams are omitted from public release.
baselineSummary: Simple baseline: repository metadata can be read without invoking scientist orchestration.
Stronger baseline: CLI registration must exist before the route can claim runtime evidence.
negativeControlSummary: Unsafe-goal safety blocker was checked as a control in the scientist audit path.
Unsupported AGI/human-level/breakthrough wording is blocked by claim curation checks.
findings: Repo/test reproduction route can produce bounded runtime evidence for the new scientist command family.
This program is support evidence for orchestration readiness, not an external scientific discovery claim.
negativeOrPartialFindings: The repo/test route remains partial until it is applied to a third-party public repository with full test collection.

checks.finding: Baselines were documented before any positive claim.
Runtime probe metrics are limited to execution/replay status.
Leakage-like mechanisms are treated as hypotheses unless direct evidence exists.
No AGI, human-level science, guaranteed discovery, or breakthrough claim is present.
Public package stores summaries, not raw logs.

candidateProblems.title: Time-series anomaly negative-control benchmark

sourceEvidence.title: NAB Time-Series Anomaly Benchmark Challenge
UCI Bike Sharing Time-Series Holdout Benchmark
UCI Air Quality Time-Series Dataset Audit
Do provenance-aware anomaly scoring methods reduce false positives in public-weather-proxy energy datasets compared with simple threshold baselines?

candidateProblems.whyNow: Earlier dataset audits produced useful negatives but this domain has less program continuity.
candidateProblems.expectedScientificValue: Tests whether anomaly claims survive simple negative controls.
candidateProblems.falsifiability: Method claims fail if shuffled or naive seasonal baselines dominate.
candidateProblems.executionFeasibility: Feasible but target selection needs more public protocol evidence.
candidateProblems.safetyScope: safe computational research only; no medical advice, wet-lab protocol, exploit, malware, private data, or legal patentability claim

limitations: General AI Scientist v0 is a bounded computational controller, not an AGI or human-level scientist.
The trial selects and executes safe computational programs only.
Leakage-risk deltas are not treated as confirmed leakage without direct mechanism evidence.
Repo/test runtime evidence is a bounded smoke route until used on third-party public repositories.
Public artifacts contain summaries only and do not publish raw logs.
